reasoning: The 360-day average of fatalities on Nov 27, 2025 was 0.000 indicating no ACLED-recorded fatalities in Bosnia for almost an entire year. Thus, for the question to resolve to YES, there simply needs to be at least one ACLED-coded fatality in the 30-day windows prior to each resolution date. Given the extremely low base rate of political or organized armed violence in Bosnia resulting in deaths (which excludes most normal homicides), the probability of >=1 fatality in any given month is very low. For 2025-12-14, 23 of the 30 days have already passed without any fatalities recorded in news search results, leaving a very small 7-day window. For the subsequent dates, the likelihood is just the baseline monthly probability of unrest, which remains low but carries slightly more uncertainty as it extends further into 2026. Therefore, probabilities are bounded near the minimum threshold and increase modestly with time.
